narrative.overview: mlxctl is a calm, dense operational surface used while real work is happening. It favors a stable shell, restrained tonal layers, exact resource names, and direct actions.
keyCharacteristics: Evidence and remediation one action away
Color reserved for selection, status, and consequence
Familiar terminal controls with explicit labels and help

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Every colored mark communicates selection, status, relationship, focus, or consequence and has a text or symbol equivalent.

rules.name: The Flat Control Room Rule
rules.body: A panel earns another surface layer only when it changes focus, ownership, or interaction mode.

dos: Do keep the operations console stable while detail and intent flows change in the workspace.
Do label every state independently of color and keep every action available from both context and the command palette.
donts: Don't recreate the existing feature-poor dashboard.
Don't build a raw daemon console.
Don't use generic dashboard card soup, decorative terminal nostalgia, neon cyberpunk, or unfamiliar controls invented for visual novelty.
Don't build a setup wizard that hides exact revisions, trust, resource cost, or the plan it is about to apply.
